invariant_definition: A signature Viśiṣṭādvaita technical doctrine: the *jīva*'s knowledge is itself a substance — a *dharma* (qualifying feature) that is also *bhūta* (existent-as-substance) — qualifying the soul. Distinct from the soul's substantive self-luminosity (*dharmi-jñāna*). *Dharmabhūta-jñāna* contracts under *karma* (this contraction is what *avidyā* really is, in V) and expands at *mokṣa*. The doctrine allows V to retain real cognitive activity for the *jīva* while preserving the *jīva*'s individuality and finite scope.

per_school.definition: Central technical doctrine. The soul has two kinds of *jñāna*: *dharmi-jñāna* (the soul-as-knowing-substance) and *dharmabhūta-jñāna* (the substantive knowledge qualifying the soul). The latter is what extends to grasp objects; its contraction under *karma* is the mechanism of *saṃsāra*. *Mokṣa* is its full expansion toward the Lord.

translator_note: 'Substantive knowledge' or 'attribute-knowledge' both partially capture. Untranslatable in one English word. The doctrine is one of the most original technical posits in V.
